sentences.english: My favorite proverb would have to be 'Don't judge a book by its cover.' You always need to give someone a chance.
sentences.korean: 가장 좋아하는 속담은 '겉모습으로 판단하지 마라'예요. 항상 누군가에게 기회를 줘야 해요.

sentences.english: 'Don't count your chickens before they hatch' — it means don't always bank on you guessing the result.
sentences.korean: '부화하기 전에 병아리를 세지 마라' — 결과를 예측하는 데 의존하지 말라는 뜻이에요.

sentences.english: Life can change very quickly, so it's good to learn patience.
sentences.korean: 인생은 빠르게 변할 수 있으니 인내를 배우는 게 좋아요.

sentences.english: 'Hear and I forget; see and I understand; I do and I know.' Until you actually do something, it's hard to really, truly know it.
sentences.korean: '듣고 잊는다; 보고 이해한다; 하고 안다.' 실제로 뭔가를 하기 전에는 정말로 아는 게 어려워요.

sentences.english: I really like the proverb, 'The grass is always greener on the other side.'
sentences.korean: 이 속담을 정말 좋아해요: '다른 쪽의 잔디가 항상 더 푸르다.'

quiz.question: What does '속담, 격언' mean in English?
options: don't judge a book by its cover
don't count your chickens before they hatch
